100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On March 26, 1926, the British Parliament debated a proposed bill to extend the powers of the police, sparking significant public outcry. In America, the ongoing negotiations regarding the future of the Fordney-McCumber Tariff brought economic tensions to the forefront of political discussions. Meanwhile, in Germany, the government faced increasing pressure as inflation rates continued to soar, destabilizing the economy and fueling social unrest.
